hosting.name: Heroku
hosting.description: The original git-push PaaS for running web apps, now in a sustaining-engineering phase under Salesforce.

hosting.name: Koyeb
hosting.description: Serverless platform that deploys containers and apps across many global regions including GPU instances.

hosting.name: Google Cloud Run
hosting.description: Fully managed serverless platform for running containers that scale to zero on Google Cloud.

hosting.name: AWS App Runner
hosting.description: Managed AWS service for deploying containerized web apps and APIs directly from source or images.

hosting.name: Azure Container Apps
hosting.description: Serverless container platform on Azure with scale-to-zero and built-in Dapr and KEDA support.

hosting.name: AWS Lambda
hosting.description: Event-driven serverless functions that run code without provisioning servers across AWS regions.

hosting.name: Scaleway
hosting.description: European cloud provider offering serverless containers, functions, and managed app hosting.

hosting.name: Coolify
hosting.description: Open-source self-hostable PaaS for deploying apps, databases, and services on your own servers.

hosting.name: Dokploy
hosting.description: Open-source self-hostable platform that brings a Vercel-like deploy experience to your own VPS.

hosting.name: Qovery
hosting.description: Internal developer platform that deploys apps onto your own AWS, GCP, or Azure accounts.

hosting.name: Zeabur
hosting.description: Deployment platform that ships any framework or service with autodetected builds and one click.

hosting.name: Sevalla
hosting.description: Kinsta-backed PaaS for hosting apps, databases, and static sites on Google Cloud infrastructure.

hosting.name: Sliplane
hosting.description: Simple container hosting that runs Docker images on dedicated servers with predictable pricing.

hosting.name: Vultr
hosting.description: Independent cloud offering compute, GPU, and app hosting across a broad set of global regions.

hosting.name: Akamai Linode
hosting.description: Developer-friendly cloud compute and app hosting now backed by Akamai's edge network.

hosting.name: Platform.sh
hosting.description: Git-driven PaaS that builds immutable application environments from a single config file.

hosting.name: Kinsta
hosting.description: Managed application, database, and WordPress hosting built on Google Cloud's premium network.

ci-cd.name: Bitbucket Pipelines
ci-cd.description: CI/CD built directly into Atlassian Bitbucket repositories with YAML pipeline configuration.

ci-cd.name: Azure Pipelines
ci-cd.description: Microsoft's cloud CI/CD service within Azure DevOps with multi-platform agents and YAML pipelines.

ci-cd.name: TeamCity
ci-cd.description: JetBrains CI/CD server with intelligent build chains, parallel execution, and a free tier for small teams.

ci-cd.name: Drone CI
ci-cd.description: Container-native CI/CD platform that runs every pipeline step inside an isolated Docker container.

ci-cd.name: Concourse CI
ci-cd.description: Open-source pipelines-as-code system built around resources, jobs, and reproducible containers.

ci-cd.name: Argo CD
ci-cd.description: Declarative GitOps continuous delivery tool for deploying applications to Kubernetes clusters.

ci-cd.name: Dagger
ci-cd.description: Programmable CI/CD engine where pipelines are written as code and run as portable containers.

ci-cd.name: GoCD
ci-cd.description: Open-source CI/CD server from Thoughtworks focused on modeling complex pipelines and value-stream maps.

ci-cd.name: Octopus Deploy
ci-cd.description: Release orchestration and deployment automation platform spanning cloud, on-prem, and Kubernetes targets.

ci-cd.name: Spacelift
ci-cd.description: CI/CD platform specialized for infrastructure-as-code with policy guardrails across Terraform and more.

ci-cd.name: Harness
ci-cd.description: AI-assisted software delivery platform covering CI, CD, feature flags, and deployment verification.

ci-cd.name: Flux CD
ci-cd.description: CNCF GitOps toolkit that keeps Kubernetes clusters synchronized with declarative config in Git.

ci-cd.name: Tekton
ci-cd.description: Kubernetes-native open-source framework for building CI/CD pipelines as custom resources.

ci-cd.name: Depot
ci-cd.description: Remote build acceleration service for fast Docker image builds and cached GitHub Actions runners.

ci-cd.name: Mergify
ci-cd.description: Merge queue and CI optimization platform that automates pull-request batching and flaky-test handling.

ci-cd.name: Garden
ci-cd.description: Platform that spins up production-like environments on demand for development, testing, and CI with aggressive caching.

queues-and-jobs.name: RabbitMQ
queues-and-jobs.description: Mature open-source message broker supporting AMQP and multiple protocols for reliable delivery.

queues-and-jobs.name: Apache Kafka
queues-and-jobs.description: Distributed event-streaming platform for high-throughput, durable, partitioned message logs.

queues-and-jobs.name: NATS
queues-and-jobs.description: Lightweight high-performance messaging system with JetStream persistence for streams and queues.

queues-and-jobs.name: Redpanda
queues-and-jobs.description: Kafka-compatible streaming platform written in C++ that runs without the JVM or ZooKeeper.

queues-and-jobs.name: Celery
queues-and-jobs.description: Distributed task queue for Python that runs background jobs against Redis or RabbitMQ brokers.

queues-and-jobs.name: Sidekiq
queues-and-jobs.description: Redis-backed background job processor for Ruby with a fast multithreaded worker model.

queues-and-jobs.name: Faktory
queues-and-jobs.description: Language-agnostic background job server from Sidekiq's author with a web UI and retries.

queues-and-jobs.name: Hangfire
queues-and-jobs.description: Background job library for .NET that persists jobs to SQL Server or Redis with a dashboard.

queues-and-jobs.name: Apache Airflow
queues-and-jobs.description: Workflow orchestration platform that schedules and monitors DAG-based data pipelines in Python.

queues-and-jobs.name: Prefect
queues-and-jobs.description: Python-native workflow orchestration with dynamic flows and a hybrid cloud execution model.

queues-and-jobs.name: Dagster
queues-and-jobs.description: Asset-centric data orchestrator that models pipelines around the data assets they produce.

queues-and-jobs.name: Graphile Worker
queues-and-jobs.description: High-performance Postgres-backed job queue for Node.js with no extra infrastructure required.

queues-and-jobs.name: River
queues-and-jobs.description: Robust Postgres-backed background job queue for Go with transactional enqueueing.

queues-and-jobs.name: Google Cloud Tasks
queues-and-jobs.description: Managed Google Cloud service for dispatching, scheduling, and retrying asynchronous HTTP tasks.

queues-and-jobs.name: Google Cloud Pub/Sub
queues-and-jobs.description: Serverless global messaging service for event ingestion and asynchronous delivery on Google Cloud.

queues-and-jobs.name: Windmill
queues-and-jobs.description: Open-source developer platform for turning scripts into scheduled jobs, workflows, and internal apps.

observability.name: Prometheus
observability.description: Open-source metrics monitoring system with a pull model, PromQL, and built-in alerting.

observability.name: OpenTelemetry
observability.description: Vendor-neutral CNCF standard and SDKs for collecting metrics, logs, and traces across languages.

observability.name: Grafana Loki
observability.description: Horizontally scalable log aggregation system that indexes labels rather than full log content.

observability.name: SigNoz
observability.description: Open-source OpenTelemetry-native platform unifying logs, metrics, and traces in one product.

observability.name: Uptrace
observability.description: Lightweight OpenTelemetry-native observability tool for traces, metrics, logs, and service maps.

observability.name: OpenObserve
observability.description: Open-source observability platform optimized for low-cost long-term log, metric, and trace storage.

observability.name: Dynatrace
observability.description: Enterprise observability and AIOps platform with automatic discovery and Davis AI root-cause analysis.

observability.name: Splunk Observability Cloud
observability.description: Cisco-owned full-fidelity observability suite for metrics, traces, logs, and incident response.

observability.name: Elastic Observability
observability.description: Observability built on the Elastic Stack for logs, metrics, APM, and traces with powerful search.

observability.name: Chronosphere
observability.description: Cloud-native observability platform built for high-cardinality metrics and control over telemetry cost.

observability.name: Coralogix
observability.description: Full-stack observability platform with in-stream processing and indexless customer-owned storage.

observability.name: Logz.io
observability.description: Managed open-source-based observability for logs, metrics, and traces on a cloud SaaS platform.

observability.name: Sumo Logic
observability.description: Cloud-native log analytics and observability platform combining metrics, logs, and traces.

observability.name: Last9
observability.description: OpenTelemetry-native observability platform focused on controlling high-cardinality telemetry cost.

observability.name: Highlight.io
observability.description: Open-source full-stack monitoring with session replay, error tracking, logs, and traces.

observability.name: AppSignal
observability.description: Application performance monitoring and error tracking with a focus on simplicity and clear pricing.
